title: AI Solutions Architecture Agent - System Configuration
description: Read-only configuration template for engagement-specific settings. Copy and customize per engagement. Skills read this for platform, stakeholder, and constraint context.

project._comment_type: Options: web_app, mobile_app, api, desktop_app, data_pipeline, ml_model, genai_assistant, automation_system

platform._comment_target: Options: cursor, claude_projects, aws_bedrock, azure_openai, google_vertex, custom
platform._comment_region: AWS: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1, etc. | Azure: eastus, westus, etc. | GCP: us-central1, us-west1, etc.

decision_makers._comment_role: CEO, CFO, CTO, VP Engineering, VP Product, Director

contributors._comment_role: Product Manager, Domain Expert, Business Analyst, Technical Lead, Data Scientist

budget._comment_approval_process: Who approves budget increases or changes

compliance._comment_regulations: GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, PCI-DSS, CCPA, ISO 27001, etc.
compliance._comment_data_residency: Specific countries/regions where data must reside

technical._comment_existing_infrastructure: Systems that must be used or integrated with
technical._comment_technology_restrictions: Technologies that cannot be used due to policy or licensing

constraints._comment_strategic_priority: critical (top priority, resources guaranteed), high (important, resources likely), medium (standard priority), low (nice to have)

team._comment_experience_level: junior (0-2 years), mid-level (3-5 years), senior (5-10 years), expert (10+ years)
team._comment_skills: List current team skills: python, react, aws, machine_learning, prompt_engineering, etc.
team._comment_gaps: List skills needed but not currently available

preferences._comment_code_quality_standards: Style guides, linting rules, code review policies
preferences._comment_testing_requirements: Unit test coverage %, integration tests, e2e tests

risk_tolerance._comment_innovation_vs_stability: innovative (cutting-edge tech acceptable), balanced (proven but modern), conservative (battle-tested only)
risk_tolerance._comment_cost_vs_speed: optimize_cost (slower but cheaper), balanced (reasonable trade-off), optimize_speed (faster delivery priority)
risk_tolerance._comment_build_vs_buy: prefer_build (custom solutions), balanced (case by case), prefer_buy (SaaS/managed services)

output_configuration._comment_base_directory: Where agents save all generated content (requirements, architectures, prototypes, etc.)
output_configuration._comment_use_project_subdirectories: Organize outputs by project name for better organization
output_configuration._comment_timestamp_files: Add timestamps to filenames (usually false, git tracks history)

technical_references._description: Centralized technical documentation, research papers, frameworks, and design patterns. All agents MUST reference this section instead of duplicating URLs inline.
technical_references._usage: Agents should reference by key (e.g., 'See system_config.json → technical_references → anthropic_docs') to maintain single source of truth

aws_bedrock_strands.github: (Repository not yet publicly available - check AWS announcements)
aws_bedrock_strands._note: Strands SDK announced but GitHub repo not yet published as of 2025-10-13

metagpt.title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework

small_language_models.title: Smaller Language Models - Efficient Reasoning Patterns

supervisor_worker.description: Supervisor routes requests to specialized worker agents

sequential_chain.description: Agents process in ordered sequence, each consuming previous output

parallel_execution.description: Multiple agents work concurrently on independent tasks

test_time_recursive_majority.description: Generate multiple candidates, validate each, select best, recursively improve

aws_well_architected_framework._description: Authoritative reference for AWS Well-Architected Framework (6 Pillars) and Generative AI Lens. All agents MUST reference this section instead of duplicating definitions.

operational_excellence.name: Operational Excellence
operational_excellence.description: The ability to support development and run workloads effectively, gain insight into their operations, and continuously improve supporting processes and procedures to deliver business value.

security.name: Security
security.description: The security pillar encompasses the ability to protect data, systems, and assets to take advantage of cloud technologies to improve your security.
key_areas: Data encryption (at rest and in transit)

reliability.name: Reliability
reliability.description: The ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently when it's expected to.

performance_efficiency.name: Performance Efficiency
performance_efficiency.description: The ability to use computing resources efficiently to meet system requirements and to maintain that efficiency as demand changes and technologies evolve.

cost_optimization.name: Cost Optimization
cost_optimization.description: The ability to run systems to deliver business value at the lowest price point.

sustainability.name: Sustainability
sustainability.description: The ability to continually improve sustainability impacts by reducing energy consumption and increasing efficiency across all components of a workload.
key_areas: Batch processing over real-time when possible

generative_ai_lens._description: AWS Generative AI Lens provides AI-specific best practices that complement the 6 pillars

model_selection.name: Model Selection and Right-Sizing
model_selection.description: Choose models appropriate for task complexity to optimize cost and performance
best_practices: Use smaller models for simple tasks (classification, categorization)
Reserve larger models for complex reasoning and generation
Benchmark multiple models against your use cases
Consider latency, cost, and accuracy trade-offs

prompt_engineering.name: Prompt Engineering Excellence
prompt_engineering.description: Optimize prompts for clarity, efficiency, and effectiveness

rag_optimization.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) Optimization
rag_optimization.description: Optimize knowledge retrieval and integration for accuracy and efficiency
best_practices: Optimal chunk size (balance context vs. precision)

multi_agent_coordination.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
multi_agent_coordination.description: Efficient coordination and communication between multiple AI agents
best_practices: Minimize redundant API calls across agents

responsible_ai.name: Responsible AI Practices
responsible_ai.description: Ensure ethical, fair, and transparent AI systems

knowledge_base_design.name: Knowledge Base Design
knowledge_base_design.description: Structure and manage knowledge for optimal AI access and updates

assessment_scoring.scale: 0-10 per pillar, where 0 = non-compliant, 5 = basic compliance, 10 = best-in-class
assessment_scoring.overall_calculation: Average across all 6 pillars, weighted by importance if specified

_usage_notes.purpose: This file is READ-ONLY for AI agents. It provides system-wide configuration that informs all architecture and design decisions.
_usage_notes.update_process: AI engineers update this file manually or through human-in-the-loop interactions with agents.
_usage_notes.version_control: Track changes in git to maintain configuration history.
_usage_notes.validation: Ensure all required fields are populated before beginning architecture design.
_usage_notes.well_architected_reference: All agents MUST reference aws_well_architected_framework section instead of duplicating pillar definitions inline.

project.name: Customer Support AI Assistant
project.description: AI-powered chatbot to handle tier 1 customer support inquiries

decision_makers.name: Jane Smith
decision_makers.communication_preference: weekly status emails, monthly review meetings

technology_restrictions: Must use AWS services when possible

team.training_budget: $10,000 for prompt engineering and LLM training

preferences.code_quality_standards: PEP 8 for Python, ESLint for JavaScript, 80% test coverage
preferences.documentation_requirements: Inline code docs, architecture docs, API docs, user guides
preferences.testing_requirements: Unit tests (80% coverage), integration tests, e2e tests for critical paths
preferences.ci_cd_preferences: GitHub Actions, automated deployment to staging, manual approval for production
